story.iWant: the CLI commands (start.tsx and server.ts) to use config-driven multi-agent resolution instead of a hardcoded ClaudeAgentProvider, with proper diagnostics queue lifecycle and cost tracking
story.soThat: the CLI honors the agents config, tracks costs to disk, and flushes telemetry before shutdown without data loss

task.title: Wire config-driven agent setup in start.tsx (replace hardcoded agent)

tasks.task: - [ ] Replace `import { ClaudeAgentProvider } from '@tamma/providers'` with imports for RoleBasedAgentResolver, AgentProviderFactory, ProviderHealthTracker, AgentPromptRegistry - [ ] Import type IRoleBasedAgentResolver from '@tamma/providers' - [ ] Import normalizeAgentsConfig from '../config.js' (re-exported from @tamma/shared per Story 9-1) - [ ] Import DiagnosticsQueue, createDiagnosticsProcessor from '@tamma/shared' - [ ] Import ContentSanitizer from '@tamma/shared/security' - [ ] Import createCostTracker, FileStore from '@tamma/cost-monitor' - [ ] Replace `const agent = new ClaudeAgentProvider()` with config-driven setup - [ ] Build costStorePath using `path.join(config.engine.workingDirectory, '.tamma', 'cost-data.json')` - [ ] Create FileStore with plain string path (NOT object) - [ ] Create DiagnosticsQueue with drainIntervalMs: 5000, maxQueueSize: 1000 - [ ] Wire diagnosticsQueue.setProcessor with createDiagnosticsProcessor(costTracker, logger) - [ ] Create ContentSanitizer conditionally based on config.security?.sanitizeContent - [ ] Log info message when ContentSanitizer is created (default-on) - [ ] Create RoleBasedAgentResolver with options object, typed as IRoleBasedAgentResolver - [ ] Pass agentResolver (typed as IRoleBasedAgentResolver, not agent) to both TammaEngine constructor calls (service mode and interactive mode)

task.title: Wire config-driven agent setup in server.ts

tasks.task: - [ ] Replace `import { ClaudeAgentProvider } from '@tamma/providers'` with multi-agent imports - [ ] Import type IRoleBasedAgentResolver from '@tamma/providers' - [ ] Import normalizeAgentsConfig from '../config.js' (re-exported from @tamma/shared per Story 9-1) - [ ] Import DiagnosticsQueue, createDiagnosticsProcessor from '@tamma/shared' - [ ] Import ContentSanitizer from '@tamma/shared/security' - [ ] Import createCostTracker, FileStore from '@tamma/cost-monitor' - [ ] Replace `const agent = new ClaudeAgentProvider()` with config-driven setup (same pattern as start.tsx) - [ ] Log info message when ContentSanitizer is created (default-on) - [ ] Pass agentResolver (typed as IRoleBasedAgentResolver) to TammaEngine constructor - [ ] Add diagnosticsQueue and costTracker disposal to shutdown handler with try/catch, re-entrancy guard, and timeout

- [ ] Add `await diagnosticsQueue.dispose()` BEFORE `process.exit(0)` in service mode shutdown (start.tsx) - [ ] Add `await costTracker.dispose()` AFTER diagnosticsQueue.dispose() in service mode shutdown - [ ] Add `await diagnosticsQueue.dispose()` BEFORE `process.exit(0)` in interactive mode shutdown (start.tsx) - [ ] Add `await costTracker.dispose()` AFTER diagnosticsQueue.dispose() in interactive mode shutdown - [ ] Add `await diagnosticsQueue.dispose()` BEFORE `await app.close()` in server.ts shutdown - [ ] Add `await costTracker.dispose()` AFTER diagnosticsQueue.dispose() in server.ts shutdown - [ ] Wrap each disposal call in try/catch so a single failure does not prevent subsequent disposals - [ ] Add `shuttingDown` re-entrancy guard to all three shutdown handlers - [ ] Add 10-second unref'd shutdown timeout to all three shutdown handlers - [ ] Ensure dry-run mode disposes diagnosticsQueue and costTracker before exit - [ ] Ensure interactive mode early-exit paths dispose diagnosticsQueue and costTracker - [ ] Verify: process.on('beforeExit') is NOT used (it never fires with process.exit(0))

task.title: Add cost-monitor dependency and ensure normalizeAgentsConfig is exported

tasks.task: - [ ] Add `"@tamma/cost-monitor": "workspace:*"` to packages/cli/package.json dependencies - [ ] Verify normalizeAgentsConfig() is exported from packages/cli/src/config.ts (re-exported from @tamma/shared per Story 9-1) - [ ] Verify Story 9-1's mergeConfig() fix handles config.agents and config.security sections - [ ] Run pnpm install to update lockfile

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: start.tsx no longer imports or instantiates ClaudeAgentProvider directly; uses RoleBasedAgentResolver constructed from normalizeAgentsConfig() output
server.ts no longer imports or instantiates ClaudeAgentProvider directly; uses same config-driven agent setup pattern as start.tsx
TammaEngine receives `agentResolver` (not `agent`) in both start.tsx (service + interactive modes) and server.ts
Cost data saved to `.tamma/cost-data.json` under config.engine.workingDirectory after a run
FileStore constructed with plain string path `costStorePath`, not an object like `{ filePath }`
`diagnosticsQueue.dispose()` called BEFORE `process.exit(0)` in all three shutdown handlers (start.tsx service, start.tsx interactive, server.ts)
`costTracker.dispose()` called AFTER `diagnosticsQueue.dispose()` and BEFORE `process.exit(0)` in all three shutdown handlers
`process.on('beforeExit')` is NOT used anywhere for disposal (it never fires with `process.exit(0)`)
Legacy config with only `agent` field works via `normalizeAgentsConfig()` producing a single-entry provider chain
New config with `agents` field uses role-based resolution with per-role provider chains
`@tamma/cost-monitor` listed as dependency in packages/cli/package.json
Server shutdown order: engineRegistry.disposeAll() -> diagnosticsQueue.dispose() -> costTracker.dispose() -> app.close() -> process.exit(0)
ContentSanitizer created only when `config.security?.sanitizeContent !== false`
Each disposal call in shutdown handlers is wrapped in try/catch so a single failure does not prevent subsequent disposals
Shutdown handlers include a `shuttingDown` re-entrancy guard — second signal causes immediate process.exit(1)
Shutdown handlers set a 10-second unref'd timeout that forces process.exit(1) if disposal hangs
`agentResolver` is typed as `IRoleBasedAgentResolver` (not the concrete class) when passed to TammaEngine
When ContentSanitizer is created, an info-level log message is emitted
Dry-run mode disposes diagnosticsQueue and costTracker before exit
Interactive mode early-exit paths (no candidates, user skips) dispose diagnosticsQueue and costTracker

docs.doc: Story specification with exact code snippets for all modifications
Epic 9 overview with dependency graph showing Story 10 depends on Stories 1, 9, 11a, 11b
Engine integration story — EngineContext accepts agentResolver instead of agent
Configuration schema story — normalizeAgentsConfig(), AgentsConfig types
Technical architecture (TammaConfig, event sourcing, naming conventions)
Project coding standards, TypeScript strict mode, naming conventions

code.file: Replace hardcoded ClaudeAgentProvider with config-driven RoleBasedAgentResolver; fix shutdown to dispose diagnosticsQueue and costTracker before process.exit(0)
Replace hardcoded ClaudeAgentProvider with config-driven RoleBasedAgentResolver; add diagnosticsQueue and costTracker disposal to shutdown
Ensure normalizeAgentsConfig() is exported (may already be from Story 9-1)

constraints.constraint: Legacy config with only `agent` field must continue to work; `normalizeAgentsConfig()` bridges it to AgentsConfig
FileStore constructor takes a plain string (filePath: string), NOT an object — see file-store.ts line 39
process.on('beforeExit') NEVER fires when shutdown() calls process.exit(0); disposal must happen inside shutdown() before process.exit(0)
`cwd` does not exist in start.tsx scope; use `config.engine.workingDirectory` instead
The diagnostics queue class is `DiagnosticsQueue` from `@tamma/shared`, NOT `ToolHookRegistry` from mcp-client
The prompt registry class is `AgentPromptRegistry` (NOT `PromptRegistry`) to avoid collision with MCP PromptRegistry in packages/mcp-client
RoleBasedAgentResolver uses options-object constructor (RoleBasedAgentResolverOptions), not positional parameters
All code must compile under TypeScript strict mode
Use .js extensions in import paths for ESM compatibility
No API keys or secrets in default config values or test fixtures
Disposal order: engine.dispose() -> diagnosticsQueue.dispose() -> costTracker.dispose() -> process.exit(0)
Each disposal call in shutdown must be wrapped in try/catch so a single failure does not prevent subsequent disposals or process exit
Shutdown handlers must use a `shuttingDown` guard — second signal causes immediate process.exit(1)
Shutdown must set a 10-second timeout (unref'd) that forces process.exit(1) if disposal hangs
The CLI creates a concrete RoleBasedAgentResolver but passes it to the engine typed as IRoleBasedAgentResolver per Story 9-9's DIP requirement
Story 9-1's mergeConfig() fix must be implemented before or alongside Story 9-10, otherwise config.agents and config.security will be silently dropped during config loading
When ContentSanitizer is created (default-on), log an info message: 'Content sanitization enabled (default-on; set security.sanitizeContent=false to disable)'
Dry-run mode must dispose diagnosticsQueue and costTracker before exit
Interactive mode early-exit paths (no candidates, user skips) must dispose diagnosticsQueue and costTracker

interface.name: RoleBasedAgentResolver
interface.description: Resolves workflow phase to agent role to provider chain. Constructor takes RoleBasedAgentResolverOptions object with config, factory, health, promptRegistry, diagnostics, costTracker?, sanitizer?, logger?

interface.name: RoleBasedAgentResolverOptions
interface.description: Options object: { config: AgentsConfig, factory: IAgentProviderFactory, health: IProviderHealthTracker, promptRegistry: IAgentPromptRegistry, diagnostics: DiagnosticsQueue, costTracker?: ICostTracker, sanitizer?: IContentSanitizer, logger?: ILogger }

interface.name: IRoleBasedAgentResolver
interface.description: Interface for RoleBasedAgentResolver. The CLI creates a concrete RoleBasedAgentResolver but passes it to the engine typed as IRoleBasedAgentResolver per Story 9-9's DIP requirement.

interface.name: AgentProviderFactory
interface.description: Creates IAgentProvider by name string. Has register() and create() methods. Pre-registers claude-code, opencode, openrouter, zen-mcp

interface.name: ProviderHealthTracker
interface.description: Tracks provider health status (success/failure counts, circuit breaker state). Used by ProviderChain for failover decisions

interface.name: AgentPromptRegistry
interface.description: Resolves prompt templates with fallback: per-provider-per-role -> per-role -> per-provider-default -> global default -> built-in. Has render(role, providerName, vars) method

interface.name: DiagnosticsQueue
interface.description: Async event queue for telemetry. Constructor takes { drainIntervalMs, maxQueueSize }. Has setProcessor(), emit(), dispose() methods

interface.name: createDiagnosticsProcessor
interface.description: Factory function: createDiagnosticsProcessor(costTracker, logger) -> DiagnosticsEventProcessor. Maps DiagnosticsEvent to costTracker.recordUsage()

interface.name: ContentSanitizer
interface.description: Sanitizes content for security (strips secrets, validates URLs). No-arg constructor

interface.name: FileStore
interface.description: File-based ICostStorage. Constructor: new FileStore(filePath: string, autoFlushIntervalMs?: number). Has flush(), load(), dispose() methods

interface.name: createCostTracker
interface.description: Factory: createCostTracker(options?: CostTrackerOptions) -> CostTracker. Options has storage?: ICostStorage field

interface.name: normalizeAgentsConfig
interface.description: Bridges legacy single-agent config to AgentsConfig. Returns config.agents when present; derives from config.agent via LEGACY_PROVIDER_MAP otherwise. Re-exported from @tamma/shared per Story 9-1.

interface.name: TammaEngine (EngineContext)
interface.description: After Story 9-9, EngineContext accepts optional agent?: IAgentProvider and optional agentResolver?: IRoleBasedAgentResolver (at least one required)

standards.standard: 80% line coverage, 75% branch coverage, 85% function coverage
TDD: write tests before or alongside implementation
Mock external dependencies; no real API calls in unit tests

locations.location: Tests for config-driven agent setup and shutdown lifecycle in start command
Tests for config-driven agent setup and shutdown lifecycle in server command

ideas.idea: Test that start.tsx with legacy `agent` config creates a RoleBasedAgentResolver with single-entry provider chain via normalizeAgentsConfig()
Test that start.tsx with new `agents` config passes it directly to RoleBasedAgentResolver
Test that TammaEngine receives agentResolver (not agent) in service mode
Test that TammaEngine receives agentResolver (not agent) in interactive mode
Test that server.ts passes agentResolver to TammaEngine
Test shutdown in service mode: engine.dispose() -> diagnosticsQueue.dispose() -> costTracker.dispose() -> process.exit(0)
Test shutdown in interactive mode: engine.dispose() -> diagnosticsQueue.dispose() -> costTracker.dispose() -> process.exit(0)
Test server shutdown: engineRegistry.disposeAll() -> diagnosticsQueue.dispose() -> costTracker.dispose() -> app.close() -> process.exit(0)
Test FileStore is constructed with plain string path, not object
Test costStorePath is computed as path.join(config.engine.workingDirectory, '.tamma', 'cost-data.json')
Test ContentSanitizer is created when config.security?.sanitizeContent is undefined (default true)
Test ContentSanitizer is NOT created when config.security?.sanitizeContent is false
Test DiagnosticsQueue is created with correct options (drainIntervalMs: 5000, maxQueueSize: 1000)
Test diagnosticsQueue.setProcessor is called with createDiagnosticsProcessor result
Test that process.on('beforeExit') is NOT used for disposal
Test shutdown re-entrancy guard: second signal causes immediate process.exit(1)
Test shutdown timeout (10s) forces process.exit(1) if disposal hangs
Test each disposal call is wrapped in try/catch — failure of one does not prevent others
Test that ContentSanitizer creation logs info message when enabled
Test that agentResolver is typed as IRoleBasedAgentResolver when passed to TammaEngine
Test dry-run mode disposes diagnosticsQueue and costTracker before exit
Test interactive mode early-exit paths (no candidates, user skips) dispose diagnosticsQueue and costTracker
